nativeRange.habitat: Warm springs, weedy canals, ditches, slow-moving lowland streams from southern Tamaulipas to northern Veracruz on the Atlantic slope

diet.notes: Readily accepts high-quality flake and pellet foods; benefits from variety including frozen or live foods such as bloodworms and brine shrimp, along with vegetable matter. Tends to graze on algae and plant matter in addition to meaty foods.

imageCandidates.notes: Konstantin Gerasimov; ~2149KB. Two vibrant males in a planted aquarium showing iridescent green-gold bodies and bright red-orange caudal fins; sharp, shows multiple individuals for this shoaling livebearer.
Marrabbio2; 1180x696. Sharp single male in a planted tank, iridescent yellow-green flank with orange caudal fin; whole fish well framed.
Dunkelfalke; 800x600. Sharp 'parrot' color morph, yellow-green body with vivid red-orange tail; illustrates the species' selectively bred color variation.

summary: The Variatus Platy is a peaceful, beginner-friendly livebearer from Mexico, notable for its tolerance of cooler water compared to most livebearers. Wild populations show striking color variation, and captive trade stocks include numerous selectively bred color morphs.
careNotes: The Variatus Platy (Xiphophorus variatus) is endemic to the Atlantic slope of Mexico, from southern Tamaulipas to northern Veracruz, and is a distinct species from the common Platy (X. maculatus). It tends to be slightly larger and more elongate in body form, and is generally considered more cold-tolerant than its close relative, reportedly doing well at room temperature without supplemental heating in temperate climates. Wild populations display extensive natural color variation; captive trade stocks include numerous selectively bred color morphs sold under names such as Sunset Platy, Variegated Platy, and others. In captivity, the species is known to hybridize with X. maculatus (common Platy) and X. helleri (Swordtail) — keepers wishing to maintain pure strains should house them separately. Males have been known to harass females persistently; maintaining at least 2–3 females per male is generally advised to distribute attention. The Variatus Platy is not strongly demanding about water chemistry, but tends to do best in moderately hard, slightly alkaline conditions — soft or acidic water is generally not recommended. Many commercially produced specimens have been reported to show reduced hardiness compared to wild-type or quality-bred stock, likely due to inbreeding pressure in mass production.
breedingNotes: The Variatus Platy is a prolific livebearer, with females giving birth to live fry roughly every 4–7 weeks. Broods have been reported to exceed 100 fry. Females can store sperm for several months, meaning a single mating may produce multiple successive broods. Fry are large enough at birth to accept newly hatched brine shrimp and finely crushed flake food. No parental care is provided and adults may eat fry; dense planting or a separate breeding tank is generally advised for raising fry to adulthood.

primary.name: SeriouslyFish
primary.notes: Primary care reference for this entry; provided taxonomy, water parameters, sizing, tank dimensions, and breeding information.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked scientific name, family, order, native range (confirmed endemic Mexico, Tamaulipas to Veracruz), adult size (max 7 cm), and temperature range (15–25°C). FishBase temperature floor of 15°C is lower than hobby sources; averaged with hobby guidance to yield 18°C floor.

additional.name: aqua-fish.net
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (6–7 cm), temperature (21–26°C), pH (7–8), hardness (7–26 dH), swim zone (middle), lifespan (3–5 years), and diet. Consistent with primary on all high-stakes fields.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op
additional.notes: General platy care guide with variatus-specific notes on cold-tolerance; confirmed tank size guidance (10–20 gal / 38–76 L), pH tolerance (6.8–8.5), preference for harder water, omnivore diet, beginner care level, and 3–4 year lifespan range.

fish.breedingNotes: The Variatus Platy breeds readily in most community tank conditions. For many keepers, controlling reproduction is the more common challenge — separating the sexes or rehoming fry is often necessary to manage population growth.
